untranslatable_reason: Signal requires fair value calculation (index + carry costs) and asymmetric thresholds (buy_threshold_points vs sell_threshold_points with asymmetry_ratio). Fair value is not an allowed indicator and cannot be derived from the allowed indicator set. Execution cost asymmetry requires external parameters not available in standard OHLCV data.
translation_notes: This is a fundamental arbitrage signal (cash-and-carry) that depends on calculating fair value from index components and cost-of-carry. The threshold of 50 points mentioned in source text represents a minimum deviation required to cover execution costs. Without fair_value, index data, and carry cost inputs, the rule cannot be operationalized in the backtest engine. A partial implementation would require: (1) fair value as an external data feed, (2) parameterizable buy/sell thresholds, and (3) cost-of-carry calculation logic.
